architecture.summary: DPRM trains two implicit process reward models (KG-PRM and CoT-PRM) that derive step-level rewards from outcome signals via reward parameterization, without explicit step-level annotations. KG-PRM learns structural constraints from knowledge graph preference pairs; CoT-PRM learns topological patterns through co-training on KG-derived data. During reasoning, both PRMs evaluate intermediate steps (KG triples and CoT steps) using Best-of-N selection, with KG paths guiding CoT generation and entities in CoT steps guiding next KG path selection in an iterative loop until a final answer is reached.
trained_components: KG-PRM (implicit process reward model for knowledge graph paths)
CoT-PRM (implicit process reward model for chain-of-thought steps)

biblio.title: DPRM: A Dual Implicit Process Reward Model in Multi-Hop Question Answering

baseline.name: best baseline (ImplicitPRM-DPO on WebQSP or RoG on CWQ)

claims.statement: DPRM achieves up to 16.6% improvement on Hit@1
On WebQSP, DPRM achieves F1 score of 0.7228 and Hit@1 of 0.8715
On CWQ, DPRM achieves F1 score of 0.5988 and Hit@1 of 0.7301
Removing co-training drops F1 on CWQ by 0.0569 and Hit@1 by 0.0477
DPRM achieves 92.3% of full-data performance using only one-eighth of the training data (1,000 preference pairs per PRM)
KG-PRM learns to capture topological constraints for long chain reasoning via path preference pairs that include factual errors, logical errors, and logical break patterns
Co-training enables CoT-PRM to capture topological patterns for long-chain reasoning and enables KG-PRM to learn from relational paths absent in the original KG

Implicit PRMs can derive step-level process rewards from outcome signals through reward parameterization: process reward at step equals the difference between exponential averages of outcome reward, without explicit step-level annotations
